112819.explanation: The council must be appointed based on quality, not gender.

112820.explanation: A referendum gives residents direct influence on decisions, resulting in policies that better fit and increased trust in governance. For example, regarding Hoogkarspel-South or the Zuiderdijk.

112823.explanation: Allowing farmers to open a shop or campsite strengthens the rural economy and social fabric. It provides extra income, promotes local products, and is important for villages without shops to maintain basic amenities.

112824.explanation: Municipal support makes joint energy projects feasible and strengthens cooperation to become more energy efficient.

112826.explanation: Asylum reception is a legal task. In previous decision-making, we proposed reconsideration if the distribution law changes.

112832.explanation: A nice alternative after the national ban.

112835.explanation: This is a task for the entrepreneurs themselves; the municipality can supervise this.

112836.explanation: Of course, this must comply with the applicable rules and procedures.

112841.explanation: This exceeds local politics and is not a task of the municipality.

112844.explanation: We prioritize appropriate housing within existing villages and no large-scale construction (+3000 houses) outside these villages, such as in Hoogkarspel-South.

112845.explanation: Socially meaningful work promotes reintegration and prevents loneliness, enabling people to actively participate in society again.

112847.explanation: This ensures that more residents who struggle to make ends meet receive timely support.

112849.explanation: This requires a careful analysis, as it reduces the number of regular parking spaces.

112851.explanation: In times of tight healthcare, everyone should be grateful for the care that can be provided.

112853.explanation: If the budget allows, this should flow back to the residents.

112860.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land reduce space for food production and harm the landscape; sustainable energy belongs on roofs, not on fields.

112862.explanation: With school swimming, we can make even better use of our two outdoor swimming pools.

112863.explanation: This is not a primary task for primary education; West Frisian is learned at home and in the village.

112866.explanation: If it helps alleviate the housing shortage, room rental should be allowed, provided it is within applicable rules and supervision.

112867.explanation: Every village deserves its own village center as a meeting place with facilities such as a community center, square, youth center, and neighborhood center; this strengthens social cohesion.

112868.explanation: There must be a clear evaluation of how to manage parking pressure.

112869.explanation: Maintaining bus lines is not a municipal task; the municipality would do better to lobby the province and carriers for good accessibility.

112870.explanation: We adhere to the housing agreement, currently around 60-70%.

112872.explanation: In previous decision-making, the CDA submitted a motion to have all additional costs (supervision, safety) paid by the COA.

112819.explanation: With the caveat that quality comes first. It is a good aspiration. And if a candidate has more quality/capabilities and therefore the gender ratio is no longer equal, we find quality more important. We would like to see more women active in politics and progressing!

112820.explanation: With the caveat that not all subjects are suitable for referenda. Various issues that concern a village can definitely be tested by referendum. It is important to clarify expectations beforehand: is the referendum binding or advisory? So it is clear!

112823.explanation: We are strong supporters of that. Not only does it allow farmers to undertake in a different way, it also means that people in villages don’t have to go to the supermarket for small groceries. Additionally, you buy local and fresh there, which is of course much better and healthier!

112824.explanation: It depends on the context, what is sustainable energy? There are already (commercial) companies supporting people via subsidies. We do support initiatives that actually contribute and/or save energy. The municipality can assess this per project to assist people.

112826.explanation: This is simply a consequence of national policy, which we are not strong supporters of, but we have to implement it. Whether we want to close "as soon as possible" or not is irrelevant, as we have no choice in this matter. We focus on where we have influence: safety!

112832.explanation: We think this is a good idea! In certain large villages (Venhuizen, Hoogkarspel) we see opportunities to host a nice show at a central location, which many residents will enjoy. For the other villages, this can also be discussed in consultation with local hospitality businesses or village councils to see if there is a need.

112835.explanation: Everyone should be able to be part of society, including people with disabilities. This statement is a bit simplistic in saying municipalities must always fund this. For example, a municipality cannot finance an elevator everywhere. However, a municipality can think along and help with funding.

112836.explanation: Agreed when it comes to generation homes/caregiver housing! This way you can ensure that family lives on the property, which can help a lot with the housing shortage! We are not in favor of building regular houses on the property. If you allow one, it applies to everyone. Then you lose the village character.

112840.explanation: There should be more camera surveillance at the station and places that are unsafe. Additionally, there are several locations more vulnerable to crime, where camera surveillance could also act as a deterrent.

112841.explanation: We take a neutral stance here. It depends on the size and location. Further on in smaller villages would not be ideal due to dependence on transportation. It would be more likely in Hoogkarspel, where residents should also be involved first. We value participation.

112844.explanation: We favor building within the cores, adding a courtyard or a street, or in the case of Venhuizen and Hoogkarspel, large housing developments. We cannot now oppose a new plan outside a village boundary; we first want to assess it on content and discuss it with residents.

112845.explanation: We have a nuanced view here because it depends on the context. We believe everyone can contribute to society. What is the situation of the person on welfare? Are they caring for young children? In our view, that takes priority and is socially meaningful.

112847.explanation: This is a topic we have strongly advocated for in the past, as many people can just 'fall through the cracks' regarding extra compensations. Life is expensive for many, also due to additional excise taxes from the government and waste and healthcare costs. We need to see how we can help.

112849.explanation: We receive no signals that there is a shortage here in our municipality; if signals arise, we can always address it.

112851.explanation: A noble aim, but it cannot happen "always," that is not realistic. There are major shortages of caregivers; we must cherish them and deploy them as efficiently as possible for optimal quality. Given the distances in our municipality, you cannot have multiple caregivers traveling around.

112853.explanation: We already see it as a good goal if these costs do not increase, and we want to commit to that. Reducing them is not realistic; many municipalities face financial deficits. Where would the cuts then be made? That is also not feasible. A healthy financial balance is a good aim.

112860.explanation: In principle, this is not our preference; we want the solar charger to be halted and for building on roofs to occur first. There are also plans along motorways. Our exception is if people want this, if there is local support in the village, and if there are local benefits.

112862.explanation: Schools are tasked with significantly improving the basics, such as reading and math. It is also noted that children's oral language skills are declining. Although swimming is important, all focus points cannot be addressed simultaneously at school, as this would come at the expense of the basics.

112863.explanation: We find it important that our children become acquainted with our local culture, customs, and history, and dialect is part of that. It belongs to us. That is why we have also included our election points in West Frisian. Culture connects, and we can pass this on to the children.

112866.explanation: Room rentals could solve problems in the municipality, as there are few private rental homes available due to recent legal changes. Renting rooms can also have legal advantages, for example, for people who do not want to be on one lease. It is important to monitor this!

112867.explanation: Absolutely! And a community center. Some villages already have lively community centers or cafés serving this function. We believe there should be space for large and small associations to be housed in one building; this connects people and encourages faster interaction.

112868.explanation: Currently, there seems to be enough space there. Only at certain 'peak moments' is it a bit harder to find parking. This may change in the future, requiring adjustments. Building parking spaces comes at the expense of something else (such as greenery).

112869.explanation: Absolutely, in our rural municipality many residents depend on public transport. To ensure residents can attend necessary or leisure events, good transport is needed. Good public transportation also helps combat loneliness.

112870.explanation: That percentage seems a bit high to us. This automatically leads to other homes becoming more expensive; otherwise, costs don't add up. We advocate for a lower percentage of social housing and specifically affordable owner-occupied homes; studios for starters and apartments for seniors.

112872.explanation: And not just good lighting. We find it important that safety is optimally secured. That is why we have also arranged for a 'reporting point' where low-threshold contact can be made in case of (threatening) incidents. The bike route for young students must remain safe-feeling.

112819.explanation: We are not in favor of mandates but would like to encourage it. More diversity in leadership provides a better reflection of our society.

112820.explanation: A referendum is binary (yes/no) and often lacks nuance. In our opinion, this is not the right tool to consult the population. We favor broader participation.

112823.explanation: Farm shops and farm campsites contribute to improving the livability of our municipality. We support this, provided it fits within the West Friesland Recreation and Retail Vision.

112824.explanation: This question concerns energy generation and, as far as we're concerned, there are national subsidy schemes financially available. Besides finances, Drechterland has a role in advising and encouraging green energy generation.

112826.explanation: There is no asylum reception center yet, but we believe we must comply with both the Distribution Act and provide humane shelter for people fleeing war and violence.

112832.explanation: Fireworks are harmful to air quality, people, the environment, animals, and wallets.

112835.explanation: An entrepreneur must handle this themselves due to current laws and regulations.

112836.explanation: According to Progressief Drechterland, the municipality should take control of the environment and maintain it. This prevents cluttering of the landscape, especially in our rural municipality.

112840.explanation: In some cases, extra camera surveillance can be useful, but generally, it costs a lot of extra money. There are other and perhaps better ways to improve social safety and the feeling of security.

112841.explanation: For Drechterland, this is probably not efficient. We believe this should be arranged regionally, and Drechterland could play a role in it.

112844.explanation: More building is necessary, including, for example, in Hoogkarspel-South.

112845.explanation: The reasons why people are on welfare vary. Therefore, we should not make it mandatory but do want to encourage it.

112847.explanation: Currently, we find the existing income threshold generally reasonable, but poverty is a complex problem. In some cases, we are open to additional measures but believe combating poverty should mainly have national solutions.

112849.explanation: We should encourage the use of electric transport in our living environment. Therefore, we support this, provided that charging infrastructure is also greatly improved.

112851.explanation: Freedom of choice and accessibility are extremely important. Therefore, we support this.

112853.explanation: Property tax (OZB) in Drechterland is at a very reasonable level.

112860.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land can be allowed in our view, provided this is done in consultation with residents, that residents can financially participate, that it fits well in the landscape, and that there is dual use of land.

112862.explanation: We are strong supporters of this. It is very important for the development of our youth and for safety.

112863.explanation: It is a nice idea, but schools already have many responsibilities for the students. Therefore, we can bring this to attention but cannot make it mandatory.

112866.explanation: It can contribute well to reducing housing shortages, but proper frameworks must be established, for example regarding parking.

112867.explanation: It would be very nice if every village center had a community center, but this is not equally realistic everywhere given capacity and financial resources. A municipality can support citizen initiatives.

112868.explanation: That space simply does not exist. It also comes at the expense of other functions, such as public green spaces. As far as we are concerned, we should not always encourage car use.

112869.explanation: Bus lines are a provincial matter, but if we receive more resources for this from the Province, we are definitely in favor. Public transport should be improved in our view, as this leads to less car use and more sustainable transport.

112870.explanation: Progressief Drechterland is not in favor of a minimum of 50% social or mid-level rental housing, but strongly supports at least 50% social rental and more affordable homeownership.

112872.explanation: Progressief Drechterland supports better lighting near the new asylum seekers center in Hoogkarspel. This enhances safety and the feeling of social security. The COA also covers the costs.

112819.explanation: Quality should be considered, not gender.

112820.explanation: Referenda simplify complex issues into a yes/no choice and often do not do justice to nuance. Additionally, low turnout at referenda can lead to decisions that do not represent the entire community.

112823.explanation: The word “always” causes us to disagree. We seek tailor-made solutions and maintain a positive attitude, but with conditions.

112826.explanation: We comply with laws and regulations. In accordance with the Distribution Act, we accept that an asylum seekers center will be established in our municipality.

112832.explanation: Provided there is interest and it is organized by volunteers.

112835.explanation: This is truly the entrepreneurs’ own responsibility.

112836.explanation: Under conditions, with only a company residence.

112841.explanation: Of course, we support this type of shelter and are also willing to fund it. It is important to organize this together with the other Westfriese municipality.

112844.explanation: The housing shortage is enormous and many existing villages are full. Therefore, we must look to allow housing development outside existing village boundaries or on the edges. Moreover, this creates space for new facilities and new forms of housing.

112845.explanation: We believe that people receiving benefits can and should contribute something back to society. The principle of “human scale” applies. People may be approached to do, for example, volunteer work at sports clubs or nursing homes.

112847.explanation: We find the current income limit sufficient. It is better to improve people’s own earning capacity.

112849.explanation: More electric "plug-in" cars are coming. So that is logical.

112851.explanation: Although we value freedom of choice, we believe care must meet certain quality standards and contract agreements.

112853.explanation: Lowering (costs/levels) is not realistic. Increasing is only acceptable if it adds value to the living environment for our residents.

112860.explanation: We respect the current existing agreements and designated areas for solar meadows. These were established carefully. For new search areas for solar fields within our municipality, we do not want new locations. The control and decision-making authority on this lies with the province.

112862.explanation: The responsibility of the parents, not the school.

112863.explanation: Not the task of primary schools.

112866.explanation: No slumlords in the middle of residential neighborhoods. Housing of migrant workers takes place as much as possible in comfortable accommodations near the companies. Migrant workers who want to settle permanently in our municipality are housed in regular homes.

112867.explanation: We think it is important that all villages have a meeting place and village hall. That is good for livability.

112868.explanation: Where possible, yes. In places with high parking pressure, we use space more cleverly by converting neglected green strips into parking facilities, without sacrificing valuable greenery.

112869.explanation: This is not the municipality’s responsibility but that of the province and the national government.

112870.explanation: We adhere to the housing deal of a maximum of 30%, no more. We choose more affordable owner-occupied homes.

112819.explanation: GBD stands for a college that is sound. Not selected on man or woman, but with a conscious balance, because good governance requires diversity in background, experience, perspective, and feeling.

112820.explanation: GBD wants to actively involve residents in decision-making and is exploring the local referendum as a modern participation tool, provided it is used carefully and fits Drechterland.

112823.explanation: GBD sees opportunities for farmers, but tailor-made approaches are necessary. Activities must suit the environment, traffic, and livability.

112824.explanation: Local, joint energy generation is supported, provided it is small-scale, has support, and is not on agricultural land.

112826.explanation: GBD stands for humanity and responsibility, with attention to capacity and care.

112832.explanation: GBD stands for livability, safety, and the costs of a fireworks show must be carefully weighed.

112835.explanation: GBD stands for inclusion and accessibility for everyone.

112836.explanation: Possibly through tailored solutions, fitting within spatial policy and landscape.

112840.explanation: Only deployed where it is demonstrably necessary.

112841.explanation: Safety within families should never be postponed.

112844.explanation: GBD wants to build alongside existing structures and protect the landscape.

112845.explanation: Participation is important, but always appropriate and people-centered.

112847.explanation: More people who just fall through the cracks need to be helped.

112849.explanation: GBD stands for future-proof mobility, with good distribution.

112851.explanation: Care must be tailored to the person and their background.

112853.explanation: No automatic increase, only inflation adjustment.

112860.explanation: No large-scale wind/solar from outside. First use roofs, verges, businesses. Local generation for local needs. Protect livability and landscape.

112862.explanation: Safety and equal opportunities for children.

112863.explanation: Preservation of local culture and identity.

112866.explanation: Customization needed, with attention to livability.

112867.explanation: Facilities form the heart of the community.

112868.explanation: Accessibility is important for villages and entrepreneurs.

112870.explanation: GBD applies core-specific customization, no fixed percentage.

112872.explanation: Safety and social control around an asylum seekers' center are conditional.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in preserving bus lines in the villages.
Residents of Drechterland should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Drechterland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Drechterland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Drechterland should have a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in good lighting around the new asylum seeker center (AZC) in Hoogkarspel.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include the West Frisian dialect in their teaching.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
There should be more parking spaces in the center of Hoogkarspel and the center of Venhuizen.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
For all municipal housing projects, the municipality should reserve at least 50% of the homes for social or mid-range rental.
